name: M.S. in Bioinformatics

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The 30-credit degree presents a broad interdisciplinary menu of 41 referenced courses spanning BIOL 605/630/672, CHEM 673, CS 631/634/744, DS 675/677 and a deep biostatistics spine (MATH 662->665->654/659), but requirementGroups=0 means no required core or capstone sequence is parsed, so it reads as an elective cafeteria; only CS 744 (Data Mining and Management in Bioinformatics) is a genuinely bioinformatics-specific course.
dimensions.suggestion: Define a required bioinformatics core (sequence/genomics analysis + systems biology + biostatistics) so the menu progresses coherently rather than functioning as a course catalog.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: metrics show deepestPrereqChain=2, missingCourseCount=0 and no dangling references, with clean chains such as MATH 662->665->654/659 and CS 631->632; depth is modest but sound for a menu-style master's.
dimensions.suggestion: Given the mixed BIOL/CHEM/CS/MATH intake, publish explicit bridge expectations (programming plus molecular biology) since several courses (e.g., BIOL 672, DS 644) gate only on 'permission of the instructor'.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or educational objectives appear anywhere in the provided data for this degree page.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish 4-6 measurable program learning outcomes (e.g., analyze omics data, apply statistical inference to biological questions, build reproducible pipelines).

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits=30 is standard (~10 courses), but requirementGroups=0 and creditReconcileDelta=null mean no plan-of-study split among core, electives and project/thesis (CS 700B/701B) is visible, so the balance cannot be verified.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish a plan of study that reconciles to 30 credits with an explicit core / elective / project-or-thesis credit breakdown.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: Course descriptions are present and specific across the 40 sample courses, but the page carries no parsed requirement groups, no term/section labels and no stated outcomes, so it presents as a flat course list.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a structured plan-of-study table that labels required core versus elective tracks and the culminating project/thesis.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: Quantitative currency is strong (DS 675 Machine Learning, DS 677 Deep Learning, DS 644 Big Data, CS 634 Data Mining, rich biostatistics), but modern bioinformatics staples such as next-generation-sequencing analysis, genomics algorithms and deep-learning structural biology are essentially absent, leaving CS 744 as the lone bio-specific offering.
dimensions.suggestion: Add genomics/NGS analysis and a structural-bioinformatics (deep-learning protein modeling) course to close the gap between the strong data spine and biological application.

descriptionQuality.justification: Sample course descriptions are substantive and specific (e.g., BIOL 672 modeling of metabolic/genetic networks, MATH 654 clinical-trial phases, DS 677 GPU/deep-learning topics) with no thin or placeholder entries evident.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: missingCourseCount=0 and many chains are explicit (MATH 662->665), but numerous courses list only 'permission of the instructor' and at least one entry (IS 634) has prerequisite text bleeding into the description, reducing clarity.

missingTopics.rationale: No dedicated course on alignment, assembly or phylogenetics; CS 744 is the only bioinformatics-specific offering among 41 referenced courses.
RNA-seq, variant calling and single-cell workflows are core to modern practice yet absent from the course menu.
Despite DS 677 Deep Learning, no course applies it to protein structure prediction in the AlphaFold era.
DS 644 Big Data is generic; there is no coverage of Nextflow/Snakemake or cloud genomics pipeline reproducibility.
Biostatistics (MATH 665) and ML (DS 675) exist in isolation but no course integrates genomic, proteomic and clinical data.

emergingTrends.rationale: DS 677 provides the deep-learning foundation to add AlphaFold/ESMFold-style protein-structure training the program currently lacks.
Techniques like DNABERT and scGPT could extend the ML courses toward genomic language modeling.
These now-dominant experimental data types would give the biostatistics spine (MATH 665, 678) contemporary biological applications.
MATH 654 clinical trials plus ML positions the program for translational, patient-level analytics.
Building on DS 644 Big Data to teach FAIR data and reproducible cloud pipelines reflects where computational biology infrastructure is heading.

topSuggestions: Publish measurable program-level learning outcomes for the degree page.
Define a required bioinformatics core (sequence/genomics + systems biology + biostatistics) instead of a broad elective menu.
Add genomics/NGS analysis and a deep-learning structural-bioinformatics course to close discipline gaps.
Publish a plan of study reconciling to 30 credits with a core/elective/thesis breakdown.
Introduce reproducible-pipeline and cloud-genomics content to modernize the data-engineering side.
